sosLanding.metaTitle: Under DDoS attack? 24/7 emergency support · NUO Networks
sosLanding.metaDescription: Immediate DDoS response and mitigation across layers 3, 4, and 7. Local engineering in Mexico. Activate the mitigation shield via WhatsApp.
sosLanding.headline: Under a DDoS attack? Regain control of your network in minutes.
sosLanding.subhead: Immediate response with local engineering in Mexico and the United States. Do not wait for the incident to escalate: we coordinate mitigation at network and application layers so you can operate safely again.
sosLanding.formSubtitle: Send your details and a WhatsApp number; our technical team contacts you immediately.
sosLanding.formError: Fill all fields with valid data (WhatsApp with at least 10 digits).
sosLanding.formNote: Submitting opens WhatsApp with a pre-filled message to NUO Networks México. No fee to use this channel; service scope is confirmed on the call.
sosLanding.whatsappLeadFooter: I request activation of the mitigation shield and urgent contact from the technical team.
sosLanding.tech1Body: Traffic scrubbing at the network layer, flood protection, and edge and application rules to contain the attack where it hits.
sosLanding.tech2Body: Rules tuned for critical applications and APIs, with focus on operational environments and mission loads.
sosLanding.tech3Body: Built for operators, finance, industry, and services that cannot afford prolonged downtime.
sosLanding.disclaimer: This form does not replace a managed security contract. Response times depend on availability and agreed scope.

eshopPage.seoSectionHeading: Indexable catalog: product lines and search terms
eshopPage.seoSectionBody: NUO Networks publishes this integrator-focused e-shop taxonomy in plain text for search engines and customers. It spans video surveillance (4K IP cameras — bullet, dome, cube, turret, fisheye, hidden, motorized lens, PTZ; NVRs; encoders/decoders; mobile DVRs; TurboHD TVI/AHD cameras and multichannel kits), field accessories (housings, mounts, CCTV testers, HDMI/VGA/DVI extenders and splitters, junction boxes, PTZ controllers, wireless video transmitters), structured cabling (Cat5e–Cat7A, fiber, coax, jumpers, raceways, trays, patch panels, jacks, faceplates), power (supplies, batteries, long-reach PoE, injectors, PoE switches, metered/monitored/switched PDUs, UPS, surge AC/coax/DC/network), servers & VMS (HDD, NAS/SAN, SD cards, Digifort, EPCOM Cloud), tools and video/ground testers. Access control includes panels, licenses, cables, grommets, cards/tags, door closers, REX buttons, magnetic contacts, relay cards, wireless controls, biometrics, MIFARE/iCLASS/NFC/proximity/UHF readers, standalone keypads, electric strikes, magnetic locks, emergency bars, turnstiles, flap barriers, analog/IP door phones, multi-apartment systems, vehicular gates and barriers. Further branches cover batteries, emergency lighting, grounding & lightning, grid-tie and off-grid solar, inverters, cellular 5G/4G LTE, FTTH/PON/GPON, OLT/ONT, switches and routers, outdoor racks, towers and masts, VoIP phones and gateways, enterprise Wi‑Fi, commercial audio/PA/HDMI, IoT GPS telematics, emergency fleet lighting and sirens, and marketing specials. The sections below repeat these keywords as crawlable lists.

sectors.title: Where the NUO ecosystem applies
sectors.subtitle: Scan-friendly rhythm: swipe and pick the context closest to yours.

ecosystem.title: Five integration pillars under one delivery model
ecosystem.subtitle: Logical security, patrimonial security, networking, critical infrastructure, and NUO Labs—each with named engineering owners, design documentation, and measurable acceptance criteria. Industry-standard terms (e.g. DevSecOps, Zero Trust, Cloud-Native) stay in English.

pillars.title: Cybersecurity & Logical Defense
pillars.summary: |Zero Trust|, |SOC|-style monitoring, and |pentesting| to shrink the IT/OT attack surface; L7 policies, event correlation, and response aligned with operations.

pillars.title: Intelligent Patrimonial Security
pillars.summary: |AI CCTV|, biometric access control, and integrated perimeter protection; segmentation against lateral movement toward PLC/SCADA. Catalog and support |Powered by EPCOM|.
pillars.highlight: Video and access bounded to industrial process

pillars.title: Networking & Servers
pillars.summary: |Industrial connectivity|, |fiber optics|, and switching with agreed MTTR; |high-availability servers| and resilience for mission loads without sacrificing process latency.
pillars.highlight: Transport and compute aligned to continuity

pillars.title: Critical Infrastructure
pillars.summary: |Resilient energy| (solar, |UPS|) and |mission-critical| operations; 24/7 continuity for sites, plant, and edge—where power loss is not an option.

pillars.title: NUO Labs (Digital Innovation)
pillars.summary: |Cloud-Native| delivery (|Next.js| / |AWS|), end-to-end |DevSecOps|, and |tech marketing| for digital assets—with traceability and change control.
pillars.highlight: Software with traceability and change control

nuoVsTraditional.title: The NUO edge (vs. traditional integrators)
nuoVsTraditional.subtitle: Against slow-moving integrators typical of large market competitors, NUO runs at engineering speed: deployments in weeks, IT/OT in one architecture, and software that is secure from day one.
nuoVsTraditional.aria: Comparison: slow corporates versus NUO Networks

rows.label: Response time
rows.oldWay: Month-long procurement and rollout cycles; endless meetings and scope drift quarter after quarter.
rows.nuoWay: Engineering sprints focused on critical assets: from assessment to value in weeks—with measurable milestones and minimal red tape.

rows.label: IT/OT integration
rows.oldWay: CCTV, networking, and cybersecurity split across silos and vendors that barely align—gaps between physical and logical security.
rows.nuoWay: One trust architecture: from cameras and physical access control to firewalls and Zero Trust—correlated and governed together.

rows.label: SaaS flexibility
rows.oldWay: Rigid bundles and generic licenses that do not fit the plant; little room to adapt real industrial workflows.
rows.nuoWay: Custom SaaS, dashboards, and APIs wired to your operational data and security policies from the design stage.

rows.label: Direct engineering support
rows.oldWay: Escalations through commercial layers before anyone who can fix it shows up—dead time and fuzzy ownership.
rows.nuoWay: You talk to the team that designed and deployed it: edge-to-edge engineering—assessment, rollout, and operations without extra middle layers.

convergenceItOt.title: Physical and logical security, one shield
convergenceItOt.headline: We do not just install hardware—we harden operations
convergenceItOt.lead: We integrate physical-security hardware—intelligent CCTV, access control, and intrusion—with perimeter cyber defense: next-gen firewalls, segmentation, and Zero Trust.
convergenceItOt.body: The same trust criteria govern the plant and the cloud: fewer blind spots, more traceability, and response when risk is operational—not just IT paperwork.
convergenceItOt.diagramAria: Diagram: CCTV and access control connected to a secure server and a mobile app inside a protective shield

highlights.title: Field integrations we are deploying now
highlights.subtitle: Scope summary; BOM detail, SLAs, and topology are fixed in the technical proposal.

items.title: NGFW & micro-segmentation
items.detail: L7 policies, VPN/IPsec, and OT/corporate zones with reviewable rule sets.

items.title: Fiber / transport backbone
items.detail: Ring and link design with MTTR and redundancy matched to process requirements.

items.title: NUO Labs (TypeScript/Next.js)
items.detail: APIs, dashboards, and automation wired to telemetry and B2B flows.

items.title: CCTV & physical access
items.detail: Video segmentation, IAM integration, and SOC correlation.

journey.title: Our engineering methodology
journey.subtitle: Five phases with technical deliverables and acceptance criteria—from attack surface to operations with governed telemetry.

steps.title: Diagnostics
steps.description: OT/IT inventory, attack surface, process dependencies, and segmentation baseline.

steps.title: Architecture
steps.description: Zone/conduit design, Zero Trust (identity, L3–L7 policies where applicable), and continuity paths agreed with operations.

steps.title: Implementation
steps.description: Network, perimeter security, CCTV/NVR, and services integration; controlled changes with documented rollback.

steps.title: Hardening
steps.description: Hardening, controlled testing, baseline validation, and technical handover to operations.

steps.title: Operations
steps.description: Correlated monitoring, change management, SLA metrics, and continuous threat-model refinement.

authorityPartners.title: Catalog interoperability proven in project delivery
authorityPartners.subtitle: We integrate on recognized vendors and standards. Logos are indicative via public logo services (not a substitute for official trademarks).

otCyber.title: OT segmentation, explicit trust, and minimal plant surface
otCyber.lead: IT/OT convergence without a trust model places CCTV, PLCs, and SCADA in the same risk plane as the office. NUO defines zones, conduits, and verifiable policies—every flow needs a technical justification and correlatable telemetry.

points.title: Video on dedicated VLANs with trust policies
points.body: NVR/DVR and camera isolation, administrative access control, firmware hardening, and rules that block lateral movement from video into OT or corporate.

points.title: PLCs, fieldbuses, and bounded OT cells
points.body: IEC 62443-style zone/conduit modeling, L3/L4 filtering where the process allows, live asset inventory, and containment when traffic deviates from baseline.

points.title: Zero Trust as a design constraint—not a tagline
points.body: Explicit policies for identities and devices; continuous verification across layers the operation can sustain and audit—not “VPN and done.”

points.title: SOC with OT/IT context
points.body: Same correlations for perimeter, video, and industrial links; playbooks agreed with maintenance to respond without unnecessary line shutdowns.

otCyber.disclaimer: Applicable regulation, vendors, and final topology are fixed in the statement of work; this is NUO’s engineering frame.

socDashboard.title: SOC-style preview: telemetry and alerts in one pane
socDashboard.subtitle: Operations engineering: KPIs, alert queues, and trend panels—not a one-off install. Illustration only; no customer telemetry.
socDashboard.simDisclaimer: Fictional data for illustration. In production we wire your real sources and agreed rules.

softwareHardware.title: We ship product; we do not only configure boxes
softwareHardware.lead: Clients should see two clear offers: integrating catalog gear and software layers NUO builds and runs as SaaS or custom dashboards.
hardwareBullets: Selection of firewalls, switching, CCTV, and links for your plant and carriers.
Rollout, segmentation, and policies aligned to Zero Trust and continuity.
Direct engineering support on what we deploy, with living documentation.
softwareBullets: First-party panels, APIs, and microservices: observability, MarTech, and B2B flows.
Hardened code (TypeScript/Next.js) with controlled, versioned releases.
Integration to your operational data—from network insight to governed actions.

cards.title: Secure logistics
cards.body: Dock and yard CCTV, access control, resilient links, and segmentation so an office incident does not stop the supply chain.

cards.title: Manufacturing 4.0
cards.body: PLCs, sensors, and lines protected with OT zones, IT correlation, and continuous monitoring without sacrificing process latency.

cards.title: Hardened enterprises
cards.body: HQs and data paths with SD-WAN, Zero Trust, and consistent policy across cloud, remote users, and plant visits.

quotes.quote: Resilience is not a hotfix: it is the choice to assume failure and contain it before it stops the line.
If you cannot observe it with OT/IT context, you cannot defend it: telemetry and traceability are part of design, not an add-on.
Less surface, more clarity: every port and flow needs an owner and a measurable purpose.

threatIntel.title: Recent context (Mexico)
threatIntel.subtitle: Links to curated Google News searches; NUO does not edit those outlets. They keep the technical conversation current.
threatIntel.disclaimer: Headlines describe each search focus. Results are third-party and change over time. For tailored analysis, book time with engineering.

items.title: Ransomware and extortion targeting businesses in Mexico

whyNuo.title: We do not just install hardware: we design ecosystems where every layer is born protected.
whyNuo.p1: We combine world-class vendor catalogs with Zero Trust architecture, industrial networks, and a software lab to turn operational data into decisions.
whyNuo.p2: We believe operational efficiency and close attention make the difference when risk is real. That is why we work with an engineering cadence: clear, measurable, no fluff.

cpa10Modal.headline: Schedule your CPA-10 assessment today
cpa10Modal.subhead: CPA-10 (Cyber-Physical Audit - 10) is NUO Networks' own framework built for the Mexican industrial market.
cpa10Modal.frameworkIntro: It is not an externally imposed international standard. It is our authored service-mark methodology to diagnose your IT + OT attack surface across 10 critical pillars. CPA-10 aligns with ISO 27001 and IEC 62443 control families, while remaining optimized for agile implementation across IT/OT infrastructure.

pillars.title: Logical perimeter
pillars.detail: Firewall posture and traffic filtering rules.

pillars.title: Physical perimeter
pillars.detail: Effectiveness of AI cameras and access-control barriers.

pillars.title: OT network
pillars.detail: Industrial device isolation to prevent lateral movement.

pillars.title: Resilience
pillars.detail: UPS and solar continuity response during outages.

pillars.title: Endpoints
pillars.detail: Antivirus/EDR protection across servers and workstations.

pillars.title: Data
pillars.detail: How sensitive files are stored and transmitted (AES-256).

pillars.title: Applications
pillars.detail: Vulnerability assessment for web apps and SaaS software.

pillars.title: Human factor
pillars.detail: Phishing tests and digital-security culture maturity.
